title: DIY Wall-Mounted Yard Tool Organizer
summary: Starting to rake fall leaves and realize you need a better way to store your outdoor lawn and garden tools?
description: Starting to rake fall leaves and realize you need a better way to store your outdoor lawn and garden tools? Look no further than this DIY wall-mounted yard tool organizer!

materials.name: Box of 2 ½” pocket hole screws (see tools list)

steps.title: Step one- Build two panels
steps.body: -Start by drilling two pocket holes in each end of the four Panel Sides. -Using wood glue and 2 1/2" pocket hole screws, attach two Panel Sides at the end of the long edge of a Panel Top. -Repeat to add a Panel Bottom to the other end of the Panel Sides. -Repeat these steps to create a second, identical panel.

steps.title: Step two- Attach base
steps.body: -Using a table saw, cut a 5 degree bevel along one long edge of the Base. -Drill three pocket holes on each long edge of the base on the now shorter side of the Base. -Using wood glue and 2 1/2" pocket hole screws, place the flat side of the Base on the bottom edge of one of the Panel Bottoms and attach with screws.

steps.title: Step three- Attach dividers
steps.body: -For each of the Dividers, cut a 5 degree miter on one end. -Drill two pocket hole screws on each end of each Divider. -Using wood glue and 2 1/2" pocket hole screws, attach the flat side of two Dividers on each end of the Panel Top. -Repeat to add a center Divider and two more Dividers spaced out equally. -Note you could space these Dividers out however you'd like to fit any special needs you might have.

steps.title: Step four- Attach second panel
steps.body: -Lay out the second panel and spread wood glue on the ends of each Divider and the Base before placing it on the second panel. -Use 2 1/2" pocket hole screws to attach the Base into the second panel before attaching the Dividers. -Attaching the Dividers into the second panel is a great time to use the Kreg 90 degree pocket hole driver.

steps.title: Step five- Install on wall
steps.body: -Use 2 1/2" screws to mount your yard tool organizer into the studs in the wall. -Load up the organizer with all of your yard tools for great organization!

steps.title: Step six- Enjoy!
steps.body: Enjoy your handiwork and tag @CharlestonCrafted on Instagram with a picture!
